docs.title: 🛡️ Backend-AntiGinx
docs.text: Production-ready REST API for orchestration of web security scans. Backend-AntiGinx receives scan requests, stores scan state/results in PostgreSQL, and pushes scan tasks to RabbitMQ for asynchronous worker processing.

docs.title: 🌟 About the Project
docs.text:
Backend-AntiGinx is the API layer of the AntiGinx platform, built for reliability and integration.

  • Queue-first workflow — scan tasks are published to RabbitMQ queue scan_queue
  • Stateful scan lifecycle — PENDING → RUNNING → COMPLETED
  • JWT-based authentication — register/login/me flow for protected endpoints
  • Structured JSON API — easy integration with workers, dashboards, and CI/CD pipelines
  • Container-ready delivery — prebuilt image on GHCR + Docker Compose support

docs.title: 💻 Technologies
docs.text: Technology Purpose Details 🎯 Go 1.25 Core language Fast, compiled, production-oriented 🌐 Gin HTTP framework Routing + middleware 🔷 GORM ORM Model mapping and migrations 🗄️ PostgreSQL Persistence Scan metadata and results storage 🐰 RabbitMQ Task queue Async scan dispatch to workers 🐳 Docker Containers Multi-stage image build 🧩 Docker Compose Service run mode Simple deployment with env vars 📦 GHCR Image registry Hosted backend images 📚 MkDocs Documentation GitHub Pages publishing

docs.title: 📁 Project Structure
docs.text:
backend-antiginx/
├── internal/
│   ├── api/             # Gin router and route groups
│   ├── handlers/        # Auth and scan handlers
│   └── models/          # GORM models (Scan, ScanResult, User)
├── middleware/          # JWT auth middleware
├── docs/                # MkDocs documentation pages
├── main.go              # Application entry point
├── Dockerfile           # Multi-stage image build
├── docker-compose.yml   # Compose run config
└── mkdocs.yml           # Documentation config

docs.title: 🔌 API Surface
docs.text: Method Endpoint Description Auth GET /api/health Service health check Public POST /api/auth/register Register user Public POST /api/auth/login Login and get JWT Public GET /api/auth/me Current user profile Bearer JWT POST /api/scans Submit a new scan request Public GET /api/scans/:id Retrieve scan with results Public POST /api/results Submit worker result callback Public

docs.title: 📋 Prerequisites
docs.text: Component Version Purpose Go 1.25+ Build & run locally PostgreSQL 14+ Scan metadata and results storage RabbitMQ 3.12+ Task queue (optional) Docker 24+ Containerization Docker Compose 2.0+ Orchestration

docs.title: 📚 Documentation
docs.text:
Our documentation is comprehensive and organized into logical sections:

  • Backend-AntiGinx Documentation — full documentation with API reference, architecture overview, and setup guides.
  • Quick Start — step-by-step guides for local CLI, Docker, and Docker Compose setups.
    • CLI Guide — detailed API usage examples with curl.
    • Docker Guide — how to run the backend using Docker.
    • Docker Compose Guide — orchestrate backend with PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ using Compose.

docs.title: 💻 Quick Start — CLI
docs.text: This guide shows how to test backend-antiginx directly from your terminal using curl.

docs.title: ✅ Requirements
docs.text:
• Running backend-antiginx API (locally or in Docker)
  • PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ reachable by backend
  • Go 1.25+ (for local run without Docker)
  • curl
  • Optional: jq (for pretty JSON output and token extraction)

docs.title: Set Environment Variables
docs.text:
# from repository root
cp .env.example .env

docs.title: Edit .env with credentials
docs.text:
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/antiginx
RABBITMQ_URL=amqp://user:password@localhost:5672/
JWT_SECRET=super-secret-key

docs.title: Run API
docs.text: go run main.go

docs.title: Set API base URL
docs.text: export BASE_URL="http://localhost:4000/api"

docs.title: Test Health Endpoint
docs.text:
curl -s ${BASE_URL}/health

✅ Expect: {"message":"Running..."}

docs.title: 📖 Available API Flow
docs.text:
Step Endpoint Purpose register POST /auth/register Create account login POST /auth/login Get JWT token me GET /auth/me Validate JWT and fetch current user submit scan POST /scans Create scan task (PENDING) get scan GET /scans/{id} Read scan details and results submit result POST /results Worker-style result callback

📌 Important Notes:

  • Password for register/login must have at least 8 characters.
  • testId in /results should be the same UUID returned as scanId from /scans.
  • jq is optional; remove | jq from commands if not installed.

docs.title: 1️⃣ Register User
docs.text:
curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "full_name":"Jane Doe",
    "email":"jane@example.com",
    "password":"SecurePass123"
  }'
✅ Expect: {"message":"User registered successfully"}

docs.title: 2️⃣ Login & Save Token
docs.text:
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email":"jane@example.com",
    "password":"SecurePass123"
  }' | jq -r '.token')

echo "Token: ${TOKEN}"
✅ Expect: JSON with token and expires_in

docs.title: 3️⃣ Validate Auth (/auth/me)
docs.text:
curl -s ${BASE_URL}/auth/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" | jq
✅ Expect: user id, full_name, email

docs.title: 4️⃣ Submit a Scan
docs.text:
SCAN_ID=$(curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/scans \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"target_url":"https://example.com"}' | jq -r '.scanId')

echo "Scan ID: ${SCAN_ID}"
✅ Expect: scanId and status (PENDING)

docs.title: 5️⃣ Retrieve Scan
docs.text:
curl -s ${BASE_URL}/scans/${SCAN_ID} | jq
✅ Expect: scan metadata + results array

docs.title: 6️⃣ Submit Worker Result (Manual Simulation)
docs.text:
Normally sent by worker, but you can test manually: 

curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/results \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "target":"https://example.com",
    "testId":"'"${SCAN_ID}"'",
    "result":{
      "Name":"csp-header",
      "Certainty":90,
      "ThreatLevel":"Info",
      "Description":"CSP header present but missing frame-ancestors directive",
      "Metadata":{"header":"Content-Security-Policy"}
    }
  }' | jq
✅ Expect: {"message":"Result received"}

docs.title: 7️⃣ Mark Scan as Completed (Optional)
docs.text:
In this backend, sending an empty result.Name marks scan as completed: 

curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/results \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "target":"https://example.com",
    "testId":"'"${SCAN_ID}"'",
    "result":{
      "Name":"",
      "Certainty":0,
      "ThreatLevel":"Info",
      "Description":"",
      "Metadata":{}
    }
  }' | jq
✅ Expect: {"message":"Scan completed"}

docs.title: 🧾 Status Codes & Common Headers
docs.text: Status Meaning Action 200 OK Request successful Continue flow 202 Accepted Scan accepted and queued Poll with GET /scans/{id} 400 Bad Request Invalid payload/UUID Verify JSON and testId format 401 Unauthorized Missing/invalid token Login again and resend header 404 Not Found Scan does not exist Check scanId value 500 Internal Server Error Backend dependency/runtime issue Check backend logs Header Usage Required For Authorization: Bearer <token> JWT authentication /auth/me Content-Type: application/json JSON body All POST requests

docs.title: 🔁 Full Test Workflow
docs.text:
# 0) Base URL
export BASE_URL="http://localhost:4000/api"

# 1) Health
curl -s ${BASE_URL}/health | jq

# 2) Register
curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"full_name":"Test User","email":"test@test.com","password":"TestPass123"}'

# 3) Login
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"test@test.com","password":"TestPass123"}' | jq -r '.token')

# 4) Verify token
curl -s ${BASE_URL}/auth/me -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" | jq

# 5) Submit scan
SCAN_ID=$(curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/scans \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"target_url":"https://example.com"}' | jq -r '.scanId')

# 6) Submit one result item
curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/results \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"target":"https://example.com","testId":"'"${SCAN_ID}"'","result":{"Name":"https","Certainty":90,"ThreatLevel":"Info","Description":"HTTPS check","Metadata":{}}}' | jq

# 7) Mark completed
curl -s -X POST ${BASE_URL}/results \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"target":"https://example.com","testId":"'"${SCAN_ID}"'","result":{"Name":"","Certainty":0,"ThreatLevel":"Info","Description":"","Metadata":{}}}' | jq

# 8) Get final scan
curl -s ${BASE_URL}/scans/${SCAN_ID} | jq

docs.title: 🧹 Cleanup
docs.text: unset TOKEN SCAN_ID BASE_URL

docs.title: 🐳 Quick Start — Docker
docs.text: Run backend-antiginx in a container without a local Go setup.

docs.title: ✅ Requirements
docs.text:
• Docker 24+
  • Internet access (to pull the image from GHCR)
  • Reachable PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ instances
  • Prepared environment values: DATABASE_URL, RABBITMQ_URL, JWT_SECRET

docs.title: Option A: Pre-built Image from GHCR
Pull the image
docs.text: docker pull ghcr.io/prawo-i-piesc/backend-antiginx:latest

docs.title: Run the container
docs.text:
docker run -d \
  --name antiginx-backend \
  -p 4000:4000 \
  -e DATABASE_URL="postgres://user:pass@host:5432/antiginx" \
  -e RABBITMQ_URL="amqp://user:pass@host:5672/" \
  -e JWT_SECRET="super-secret-key" \
  ghcr.io/prawo-i-piesc/backend-antiginx:latest

docs.title: Build the image
docs.text: docker build -t backend-antiginx:local .

docs.title: Run the container
docs.text:
docker run -d \
  --name antiginx-backend-local \
  -p 4000:4000 \
  -e DATABASE_URL="postgres://user:pass@host:5432/antiginx" \
  -e RABBITMQ_URL="amqp://user:pass@host:5672/" \
  -e JWT_SECRET="super-secret-key" \
  backend-antiginx:local

docs.title: ✅ Quick Validation
docs.text:
Check container status: 

docker ps

Check API health: 

curl http://localhost:4000/api/health

View logs: 

docker logs -f antiginx-backend

docs.title: 🔍 Useful Diagnostic Commands
docs.text:
Check if image exists locally: 

docker images | grep backend-antiginx

List all containers (running and stopped): 

docker ps -a

Inspect logs for a specific container: 

docker logs <container_id>

docs.title: ⏹️ Stop Container
docs.text:
Stop and remove container: 

docker stop antiginx-backend && docker rm antiginx-backend

docs.title: 🛠️ Notes
docs.text:
• Backend listens on port 4000 inside the container.
  • The image runs as non-root user (appuser).
  • If port 4000 is busy, change mapping (for example: -p 8080:4000).

docs.title: 🔧 Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Cannot connect to PostgreSQL/RabbitMQ → Verify host, port, credentials, and network reachability from container.
  • Auth endpoints return 500/401 → Ensure JWT_SECRET is set and non-empty.
  • Using localhost in URLs fails → If DB/MQ are outside this container, use reachable hostnames/IPs (on macOS often host.docker.internal).
  • Container exits immediately → Check startup errors with docker logs antiginx-backend.

docs.title: 🧩 Quick Start — Docker Compose
docs.text: This variant runs backend-antiginx as a container service connected to PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ.

docs.title: ✅ Requirements
docs.text:
• Docker + Docker Compose (v2)
  • Running PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ instances
  • Existing Docker network antiginx (defined as external)

Create the network (one-time setup) if you don't have it: 

docker network create antiginx

docs.title: 1️⃣ Prepare .env File
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the project root directory: 

DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@dbhost:5432/antiginx
RABBITMQ_URL=amqp://user:pass@mqhost:5672/
JWT_SECRET=super-secret-key
BACKEND_PORT=4000

📋 About Environment Variables:

  • DATABASE_URL connects backend to PostgreSQL.
  • RABBITMQ_URL connects backend to RabbitMQ and publishes tasks to scan_queue.
  • JWT_SECRET is required for JWT token signing (/api/auth/login, /api/auth/me).
  • BACKEND_PORT is used for Compose mapping (${BACKEND_PORT}:4000).

docs.title: 2️⃣ Create docker-compose.yml
docs.text:
Create or update the docker-compose.yml file in your project root: 

services:
  backend-antiginx:
    image: ghcr.io/prawo-i-piesc/backend-antiginx:latest
    container_name: backend
    restart: unless-stopped

    ports:
      - "${BACKEND_PORT}:4000"

    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}
      - RABBITMQ_URL=${RABBITMQ_URL}
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}

    mem_limit: 2048m

    networks:
      - antiginx

networks:
  antiginx:
    external: true

💡 Customization:

  • Change ghcr.io/prawo-i-piesc/backend-antiginx:latest to your own image/tag if needed.
  • Adjust mem_limit based on available server resources.
  • If you do not use an external network, replace external: true with an internal network setup.

docs.title: 3️⃣ Start Services
docs.text:
Start the container in detached mode: 

docker compose up -d

docs.title: ✅ Quick Validation
docs.text:
Check status: 

docker compose ps

Verify health endpoint: 

curl http://localhost:${BACKEND_PORT:-4000}/api/health

View logs: 

docker compose logs -f backend-antiginx

docs.title: 4️⃣ Stop Services
docs.text:
Stop and remove containers: 

docker compose down

docs.title: 🔄 How It Works
docs.text:
• Backend starts on port 4000 inside the container.
  • On POST /api/scans, a scan record is created in PostgreSQL with PENDING status.
  • Backend publishes a task message to RabbitMQ queue scan_queue.
  • Workers send results to POST /api/results; backend stores them and updates scan status (RUNNING/COMPLETED).

docs.title: 🔧 Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Error: network antiginx declared as external, but could not be found → Create the network: docker network create antiginx.
  • Auth endpoints return 500/401 → Verify JWT_SECRET is set and non-empty in .env.
  • No DB/RabbitMQ connection → Check DATABASE_URL and RABBITMQ_URL, then restart with docker compose up -d.
  • localhost in DB/MQ URLs does not work → If services run in other containers/hosts, use reachable hostnames (e.g., service name, container DNS, or external host IP).
  • Container keeps restarting → Check logs with docker compose logs -f backend-antiginx and validate external service reachability.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start
docs.text: Welcome to Backend-Antiginx — an API that queues security scan tasks in RabbitMQ and stores scan data/results in PostgreSQL. Get up and running in minutes. This guide takes you from zero to a working API.

docs.title: 🧭 Choose Your Setup
docs.text: Scenario Best Path Best For Quick scan from terminal CLI Developers, Pentesters Scan via pre-built image Docker DevOps, CI/CD Backend with external network/services Docker Compose Backend / Queue-based setups

docs.title: ✅ Requirements
docs.text:
• Go 1.25+ (if running without containers)
  • Docker 24+ (for containers)
  • Docker Compose (for orchestration)
  • Available services: PostgreSQL 14+ and RabbitMQ 3.12+ (configured via environment variables)
  • Optional: jq — useful for CLI JSON output parsing

docs.title: 🔐 Environment Variables
docs.text:
Variable Description Example DATABASE_URL PostgreSQL connection string postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/antiginx RABBITMQ_URL RabbitMQ connection string amqp://user:pass@localhost:5672/ JWT_SECRET Secret key for JWT signing (required for auth endpoints) super-secret-key BACKEND_PORT Host port mapping in compose 4000

Save to .env in your project root: 

DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@localhost:5432/antiginx
RABBITMQ_URL=amqp://user:pass@localhost:5672/
JWT_SECRET=super-secret-key
BACKEND_PORT=4000

docs.title: Clone the repo:
docs.text: git clone https://github.com/prawo-i-piesc/backend-antiginx.git

docs.title: Navigate to the backend directory:
docs.text: cd backend-antiginx

docs.title: Set environment variables:
docs.text: cp .env.example .env

docs.title: Run the application:
docs.text: go run main.go

docs.title: Test the health endpoint:
docs.text: curl http://localhost:4000/api/health

docs.title: 📡 API Overview
docs.text:
Method Path Description Requires JWT GET /api/health Service status No POST /api/auth/register Register a user No POST /api/auth/login Get authentication token No GET /api/auth/me Get current user profile Yes POST /api/scans Submit a new scan No GET /api/scans/{id} Retrieve scan and results No POST /api/results Submit results from workers No

Auth flow:

  • Use POST /api/auth/login to obtain a token.
  • Use that token in Authorization: Bearer <token> for GET /api/auth/me.

docs.title: 🔧 Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• No connection to PostgreSQL or RabbitMQ — verify DATABASE_URL and RABBITMQ_URL; test port connectivity from host
  • 401 on GET /api/auth/me — ensure header Authorization: Bearer <token> comes from POST /api/auth/login
  • 500 on login/token generation — check that JWT_SECRET is set in environment
  • Port already in use — change BACKEND_PORT in .env and re-map when running Docker/Compose
  • Migration errors — drop old test tables or verify database user permissions

docs.title: 🎯 What's Next?
docs.text:
• Want full parameter docs and all available tests? → CLI Guide
  • Want to run via container image? → Docker Guide
  • Want a worker + queue setup? → Docker Compose Guide

docs.title: api
docs.text:
import "github.com/prawo-i-piesc/backend/internal/api"

Package api provides HTTP routing configuration for the backend-antiginx service.

This package defines the API routes and their mappings to handler functions using the Gin web framework.

docs.title: Index
docs.text: • func NewRouter(scanHandler *handlers.ScanHandler, authHandler *handlers.AuthHandler) *gin.Engine

docs.title: func NewRouter
docs.text:
func NewRouter(scanHandler *handlers.ScanHandler, authHandler *handlers.AuthHandler) *gin.Engine

NewRouter creates and configures a new Gin router with all API endpoints.

The router exposes the following public endpoints under /api prefix:

  • POST /api/scans - Submit a new security scan request
  • POST /api/results - Submit scan results from a worker
  • GET /api/scans/:id - Retrieve scan details and results by ID

Parameters:

  • scanHandler: Handler instance containing business logic for scan operations

Returns:

  • *gin.Engine: Configured Gin router ready to serve HTTP requests

Example:

handler := handlers.NewScanHandler(amqpChannel, db)
router := api.NewRouter(handler)
router.Run(":8080")

Generated by gomarkdoc

docs.title: models
docs.text:
import "github.com/prawo-i-piesc/backend/internal/models"

Package models defines the data structures and database models for the backend-antiginx service.

This package contains GORM models that represent database tables and are used for ORM operations throughout the application.

docs.title: Index
docs.text:
• type Scan
  • type ScanResult
  • type User

docs.title: type Scan
docs.text:
Scan represents a security scan request and its current state.

Each scan targets a specific URL and progresses through various statuses (PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED) as it is processed by worker services.

The Results field contains all individual test results associated with this scan, loaded via GORM's foreign key relationship.

type Scan struct {
    // ID is the unique identifier for the scan (UUIDv7)
    ID  uuid.UUID `gorm:"type:uuid;primary_key;" json:"id"`
    // TargetURL is the URL that was scanned for security issues
    TargetURL string `json:"target_url"`
    // Status indicates the current state of the scan (PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED)
    Status string `json:"status"`
    // CreatedAt is the timestamp when the scan was submitted
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    // StartedAt is the timestamp when a worker began processing the scan (nil if not started)
    StartedAt *time.Time `json:"started_at"`
    // CompletedAt is the timestamp when the scan finished (nil if not completed)
    CompletedAt *time.Time `json:"completed_at"`
    // Results contains all individual test results for this scan
    Results []ScanResult `gorm:"foreignKey:ScanID" json:"results"`
}

docs.title: type ScanResult
docs.text:
type ScanResult struct {
    ID       uint      `gorm:"primaryKey" json:"id"`
    ScanID   uuid.UUID `gorm:"type:uuid;index" json:"scan_id"`
    TestName string    `json:"test_name"`
    Severity string    `json:"severity"`
    Passed   bool      `json:"passed"`
    Message  string    `gorm:"type:text" json:"message"`

    Metadata datatypes.JSON `json:"metadata"`
}

docs.title: type User
docs.text:
type User struct {
    ID        uuid.UUID `gorm:"type:uuid;primary_key;" json:"id"`
    FullName  string    `json:"full_name"`
    Email     string    `gorm:"uniqueIndex; not null" json:"email"`
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    Password  []byte    `json:"-"`
}

docs.title: backend-antiginx
docs.text:
import "github.com/prawo-i-piesc/backend"

Package main provides the entry point for the backend-antiginx API server.

The server connects to PostgreSQL database and RabbitMQ message broker, performs database migrations, and starts an HTTP server for handling security scan requests.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
The following environment variables are required:

  • DATABASE_URL: PostgreSQL connection string (e.g., postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db)
  • RABBITMQ_URL: RabbitMQ connection string (e.g., amqp://user:pass@host:5672/)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
To run the server:

DATABASE_URL="postgres://..." RABBITMQ_URL="amqp://..." go run main.go

docs.title: Index
docs.text: • func main()

docs.title: func main
docs.text:
func main()

main initializes and starts the backend-antiginx API server.

It performs the following steps:

  1. Loads environment variables from .env file (optional)
  2. Establishes connection to PostgreSQL database
  3. Runs database migrations for Scan and ScanResult models
  4. Connects to RabbitMQ and declares the scan_queue
  5. Initializes HTTP handlers and starts the server on port 8080

The function will terminate with a fatal error if any critical initialization step fails (database connection, RabbitMQ connection, etc.)
